summary: Make the isolated packaged M5/M6 walkthrough address the focused workbench CodeMirror editor instead of an arbitrary mounted editor pane.

decisions.decision: Use the shared focused-workbench CodeMirror selector for every source read, replacement, element lookup, and completion-caret operation in the M5/M6 walkthrough.
decisions.rationale: History can keep read-only CodeMirror panes mounted even when they are not the active editor. Selecting the first generic CodeMirror node made the release proof nondeterministic and tested the wrong surface.

testEvidence.criterion: The M5/M6 release walkthrough must edit the active project source even when other CodeMirror panes are mounted.
testEvidence.test: Run the exact signed installer payload in the network-disabled Windows Sandbox, then protect the corrected selector with focused wiring and verifier tests.

red.outcome: RED: the base cleanroom completed 28 checks, but the separate M5/M6 runner failed its first source replacement because document.querySelector('.cm-content') selected a mounted pane without the editable workbench CodeMirror view.

green.outcome: GREEN: 2 files and 4 tests passed; the wiring regression rejects generic .cm-content selectors and requires the focused workbench selector.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
